rxn_class_summary: Paired electrophotocatalytic C(sp3)-H arylation (two-component) or C(sp3)-H alkylation (three-component, with alkene linker) of alkanes with aryl bromides, using Fe/Ni dual catalysis under blue/purple light
electrochemical_role: Anodic LMCT-driven oxidation of [FeIIICl4]- (photoexcited) regenerates FeIII from FeII while releasing Cl• that performs HAT on the alkane to give an alkyl radical; cathodic reduction generates and turns over the Ni(I)/Ni(II)/Ni(III) catalytic cycle that couples the alkyl radical with ArBr

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical R• (species E) from HAT on alkane
alkyl radical adduct onto EWG-alkene (Giese adduct, species F)

electron_flow: Under irradiation, anodic oxidation regenerates FeIII from FeII while a photoexcited [FeIIICl4]- undergoes LMCT to release Cl•, which abstracts H from the alkane to form an alkyl radical (optionally adding to an electron-poor alkene first). The cathode reduces NiII to NiI, which oxidatively adds ArBr to give NiIII-Ar; the alkyl radical is captured by aryl-NiII (or NiIII) to give a NiIII(Ar)(alkyl) intermediate that reductively eliminates the C(sp3)-C(sp2) product.
rate_determining_step: Matching of the anodic rate of alkyl radical generation (LMCT/HAT) with cathodic aryl-NiII speciation; selectivity is controlled by rate-matching rather than by a single elementary step

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; cyclohexyl-TEMPO adduct (72) detected by 1H NMR, supporting alkyl radical formation via anodic HAT

probe_reagents.name: Cycloheptyl chloride (alkyl chloride competition)
probe_reagents.observation: Reductive coupling product from alkyl chloride + ArBr (1') not detected; only cyclohexyl arylation product 1 formed (77%), excluding alkyl chloride as a key intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Ni(COD)2 as Ni(0) source
probe_reagents.observation: Only 9% yield, suggesting Ni(0) is not the operative species in the catalytic cycle

probe_reagents.name: Stoichiometric aryl-NiII complex 73 (with 6,6'-Me2-bpy)
probe_reagents.observation: With cyclohexane + FeCl3·6H2O under light gave product 1 in 37%; Ni(I) detected by HRMS, confirming aryl-NiII as catalytic species and Ni(I) intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Aryl-NiII complex 73 as catalyst (10 mol%)
probe_reagents.observation: Delivered product 23 in 46% yield, supporting catalytic competence of Ar-NiII

probe_reagents.name: Light on/off and intensity variation
probe_reagents.observation: No product without light; higher light intensity gives higher rate and selectivity (Fig. 7), consistent with photoinduced LMCT controlling radical generation rate

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of NiBr2·3H2O + bpy
probe_reagents.observation: Shows NiII/NiI reduction wave around -1.1 V vs Ag/AgCl, matching the observed cathode potential during electrolysis

probe_reagents.name: Chronopotentiometry during electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Anode held ~0.23 V and cathode ~-1.1 V vs Ag/AgCl, consistent with FeII/FeIII anodic and NiII/NiI cathodic cycles

notes: The authors emphasize 'rate-matching modulation': current (4 mA for arylation vs 25 mA for three-component alkylation) and light intensity independently tune anodic radical generation vs cathodic Ni speciation to switch selectivity between two- and three-component products. A direct anodic oxidation of Cl-/Br- to halogen radical (without LMCT photoexcitation) is explicitly excluded because the anode potential (~0.23 V) is too low. Alkyl chloride pathway and Ni(0) pathway are excluded by control experiments. The alkyl radical is proposed to be captured either by aryl-NiII (giving NiIII(Ar)(alkyl)) or by NiI followed by oxidative addition of ArBr; the favored radical-chain coupling proceeds via Ar-NiII.
